title: gutierrezia tortosae
intro: Gutierrezia tortosae, also known as California broom or greasewood broom, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the sunflower family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Gutierrezia tortosae include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of ailments, although further research is necessary to confirm its full range of benefits and potential interactions.
